name: Sorbas and Uleila del Campo Loop
name_original: Casas Colgantes de Sorbas — circular por Uleila del Campo
description: This moderate 24.4-mile touring bicycle route takes you past the unique Hanging Houses of Sorbas and through the rural Almería countryside.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Sorbas traverse a diverse landscape in the province of Almería, Spain. The region features varied terrain, from the rural Almería countryside to the unique karst formations of the Karst en Yesos de Sorbas Natural Park. Cyclists can encounter both paved and unpaved segments, with routes ranging from gentle rides to more demanding paths with significant elevation changes. The broader area also includes the arid Tabernas Desert and the rugged Sierra Alhamilla Natural Park. Best touring cycling routes around Sorbas The most popular touring cycling route is Sorbas and Uleila del Campo Loop, a 24.4 miles (39.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This route explores the rural countryside, connecting Sorbas with the traditional village of Uleila del Campo. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is In the middle of the desert – View of Playa del Algarrobico loop from Agua Amarga, a difficult 68.8 miles (110.7 km) path. This route offers extensive views across the desert landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Lucainena de las Torres – Calcination Kilns loop from Níjar, a 22.2 miles (35.8 km) trail leading through traditional villages and past historical sites, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Sorbas is defined by varied terrain, rural countryside, and karst landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Sorbas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sorbas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Sorbas?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes available around Sorbas, catering to various skill levels. This includes 6 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 38 more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Sorbas?
A: The terrain around Sorbas is quite varied. You'll find a mix of paved surfaces suitable for all skill levels, alongside some unpaved or more challenging segments. Routes often traverse the picturesque rural Almería countryside, and you can also experience the unique karst landscapes of the Karst en Yesos de Sorbas Natural Park, or even the dramatic, arid scenery of the nearby Tabernas Desert.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling around Sorbas?
A: Many routes offer views of the unique karst formations of the Karst en Yesos de Sorbas Natural Park. You might also encounter the dramatic landscapes of the Tabernas Desert and Sierra Alhamilla Natural Park. For specific points of interest, consider routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5246581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Aguas in Paraje Natural Karst en Yesos de Sorbas&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4973241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient olive tree of Agua Amarga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any charming villages to visit on a touring cycling route near Sorbas?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes connect Sorbas with traditional villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059376439/sorbas-and-uleila-del-campo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sorbas and Uleila del Campo Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the rural countryside to Uleila del Campo. Other routes might lead to villages like Bédar, Mojácar, or Lucainena de las Torres, offering glimpses into local life and architecture.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Sorbas offers 6 easy touring cycling routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature minimal elevation gain and mostly paved surfaces, allowing you to enjoy the scenery at a comfortable pace.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the Sorbas area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 38 difficult routes available. A particularly demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40390663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amethyst Viewpoint – In the middle of the desert loop from Sendero Rio Alias&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a significant distance and elevation. Another challenging route offering extensive desert views is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40387558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Playa del Algarrobico – In the middle of the desert loop from Venta del Pobre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Sorbas?
A: The touring cycling routes around Sorbas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1200 touring cyclists have explored the area, often praising the varied terrain and the authentic beauty of the rural Almería countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Sorbas?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Sorbas is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, winter months. This allows for comfortable exploration of the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Sorbas are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059376439/sorbas-and-uleila-del-campo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sorbas and Uleila del Campo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a complete circuit through the rural countryside.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Sorbas?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not always explicitly marked, Sorbas, being a town, generally offers public parking options where you can leave your vehicle before starting your cycling tour. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient spots.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Sorbas?
A: Yes, with 6 easy routes available, Sorbas offers family-friendly touring cycling options. These routes are typically shorter, have less elevation gain, and often follow quieter roads, making them suitable for families with children or those preferring a leisurely pace.
tips: Sorbas is a charming town in the province of Almería, located in the Levante Almeriense region. Known for its hanging houses and impressive caves, the town offers a unique combination of history and nature. Its monuments include the church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción, the castle of Villavieja and the Palace of the Dukes of Alba.

The hanging houses of Sorbas are a group of houses located in the historic center of the Almeria town of Sorbas. These houses are characterized by being built on the limestone of the area, forming a set of balconies and terraces that seem to be suspended in the air. Its origin is believed to date back to Muslim times, although they have undergone restorations and reforms over the centuries.

Uleila del Campo is a small town located on the southern slopes of the Sierra de los Filabres, at the foot of Monteagud Hill. Its surroundings combine hills, gullies, and unusual sites such as "El Salar," a salt flat with a unique ecosystem. Its heritage includes the Neo-Romanesque Church of Santa María and the Cueva de la Mora archaeological site, home to Neolithic remains and cave paintings.
The town keeps its traditions alive with festivals such as the Santo Cristo de las Penas festival in September, ideal for discovering local life and culture after a hike through the mountains.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Casas Colgantes de Sorbas — circular por Uleila del Campo route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on paved surfaces, making it well-suited for touring cyclists. While some variations in the broader area might include unpaved sections, this specific moderate route offers mostly smooth riding conditions. You'll cycle through diverse landscapes, including agricultural areas with vineyards, almond trees, and olive groves, set against the backdrop of the Sierra de los Filabres foothills.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, both Sorbas and Uleila del Campo are settlements along the circular route, offering potential starting points with parking options within the towns. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' and requires 'good fitness'. With an elevation gain of around 440 meters over approximately 39.3 kilometers, it includes climbs that will test endurance. Therefore, it might not be ideal for absolute beginners but is manageable for cyclists with some experience and a good fitness level.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The most notable feature is the town of Sorbas itself, famous for its 'Casas Colgantes' or 'hanging houses' perched on a ravine. You can enjoy a View of the Hanging Houses of Sorbas, often visible near the start of the ride. The route also passes through the charming village of Uleila del Campo and offers glimpses into rural Spanish life and striking natural geology.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the proximity to the Tabernas Desert, the region experiences a unique microclimate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter. These seasons also showcase the agricultural landscapes at their best.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a touring bicycle route on paved roads and through towns, dogs are generally permitted, especially if kept on a leash and under control. However, always be mindful of local regulations in towns and respect private property. Ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling speeds and road conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need special permits or to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. The trail passes through public roads and areas. However, the region is part of the Paraje Natural Karst en Yesos de Sorbas. While there are no fees for cycling through, be aware that specific activities or access to certain caves or protected areas within the Natural Park might have their own regulations or require guided tours.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Transandalus and Camino a Nuestra Señora de Monteagud. It also uses smaller local paths and streets such as Calle Garbanceros, Calle Parque, and Travesía San Francisco for short segments.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse natural features, including mountainous backdrops from the foothills of the Sierra de los Filabres. The landscape is rich with extensive fields of vineyards, almond trees, and olive groves, characteristic of the agricultural lands of Almería. The unique geology of the region, influenced by the nearby Tabernas Desert, also adds to the distinct scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route is circular and passes through the settlements of Sorbas and Uleila del Campo, you will find opportunities to stop for refreshments, food, or to refill water bottles in these towns. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially between towns.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring bicycle ride on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring bicycle route, essential items include a well-maintained touring bicycle, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, sufficient water, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses), and a fully charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation. Given the varied terrain and potential for climbs, comfortable cycling shoes are also recommended.

waypoints.name: View of the Hanging Houses of Sorbas
